_comment: Brain-root hooks. Loaded for any session opened at brain/ or below. The gielinor/.claude/settings.json holds architectural guarantees scoped to gielinor sessions; this file holds cross-brain instrumentation.
_comment_abs_path: S052: hook commands use an absolute path instead of ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}. Sessions launched from a subdirectory (e.g. switchboard/, experiments/) come up with an empty CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, which expanded to a broken '/developer-braindead/...' path and silently no-op'd every hook — that session never appeared on the switchboard or emitted events. Absolute path is machine-specific but the brain hasn't migrated hosts; revisit at ascension (death-as-migration).
_comment_status_sidecar: D-020 status-sidecar.py registered for UserPromptSubmit + Stop + SessionEnd (3 fires/turn). PreToolUse/PostToolUse NOT registered broadly — per-tool granularity isn't load-bearing and would push fire rate to 2N+3/turn. EXCEPTION (S053): status-sidecar IS registered for PreToolUse/PostToolUse matched ONLY to AskUserQuestion|ExitPlanMode — these park the session on the user mid-turn with no Stop, so the board would read WORKING while actually WAITING. Tight matcher keeps it ~0-2 fires/session. S057 EXTENDED the same matcher to Task|Agent for the waiting_for_subagents (AWAITING CREW) state — a foreground spawn blocks the parent until the crew returns; Pre sets the state, Post re-derives it. The manifest per-row override in _write_manifest is the authority (refreshes from any session's fire, self-heals a missed Post); the Pre/Post registration just covers the lone-blocked-session case. See S032 incident + S033 re-enable decision; lorebook draft on hook-fire-rate-as-budget pending.
